message: Jake
Ash
I have a favorite bar.
It's in a hard-to-find spot, without a sign, and has that feel of being a hidden gem, a nice calm place.
Before things got busy, I used to go there almost every day, but recently I've been staying away.
Long time no see,
who were you again?
Come on, don’t say something cold like that.
Hehe, it’s my fault for not showing my face for a while.
Look
Talk
Listen
Think
System
The surroundings
About the serial murder case
About the BAM article
About work
About　that
About the serial murder case
About the BAM article
About work
Save
Exit the game
It’s been a while, so please take your time. Your glass is empty. Shall I refill it?
Yeah, please.
Go ahead and drink like you're opening a whole bottle tonight.
Ugh, there’s no way I can drink that much.
It's a punishment for not coming for a while.
Ah, there's no need to refill right away.
Come on, it's fine, let’s keep going.
There’s no sound of drunk patrons shouting, and a calm time flows. The music might be a bit loud, but it’s nice that I can’t hear the voices of the neighboring customers.
Considering it's a front for a shady operation, the stock of bottles at the back bar is surprisingly plentiful.
With me and
, there seem to be only a few other customers besides the other bartender.
is the owner of this place. He has a pretty face and looks like a gentleman, but he's a mysterious person with knowledge about various things.
I've weirdly hit it off with him and have been quite friendly; he has helped me with work in the past.
As usual, when I show up, he sticks to me like my personal bartender. I seem to be
a decent way for him to pass the time.
Long time no see. I was thinking if you died of hunger, I should at least light a stick of incense for you.
That's what I was thinking.
That’s kind of ominous.
But I'm alive and well.
Hehehe, I'd rather not be a ghost.
You seem busy, which is great. I didn't have any work to introduce to you, so it worked out well. No trouble for anyone.
Is the world in a recession or something?
Oh, do you want to do a job finding dogs or cats?
No, it’s not like that.
It'd be bad to be picky.
Please take your time today.
Do you know anything about the serial murder case?
What's with the sudden question? We've never talked about that before, have we?
No, but since I went to Director Fabar’s party, it’s been on my mind.
Director Fabar's party!? I'm jealous!
Believe it or not, actresses Meryl and Jennifer were there.
Is that so!? How luxurious!
By the way, what does the party have to do with the serial murder case? I really don't get it.
Well, it came up because I'm known to be a detective and the topic of the serial murder case came up.
And then?
Someone said that Jennifer is the culprit.
What the heck is that!? There are some people who say unreasonable things.
Right?
But unfortunately, I don't know anything about that serial murder case.
I don't read those kinds of magazines, but it seems gossip about Guy was featured.
Yeah. I found out by chance, but in the end, it was written that Cindy was in a passionate romance, and Kristy became a mess.
Is that so? So, Kristy,
writers irresponsibly write things to make them sound entertaining.
What do you think about that article?
What do you think?
What kind of response was Guy giving?
What kind of response? Not really anything.
I think he probably wasn't doing anything.
It sounds as if the content of the article was approved, doesn't it?
I might be overthinking it, but isn't the series of articles related to Guy intentionally crafted with various intentions mixed in?
Deliberately created, huh?
So, Kristy got dragged into this mess?
Just remember that what I said is purely hypothetical.
How about confronting the person who wrote the article?
Nope, I actually tried, but I was completely shut down every time.
Oh dear, quite a tough opponent, aren't they? But if you can't even defend your own writing, you're not cut out to be a writer.
You've been quite busy, haven't you?
There's nothing to send to the Snipes Detective Agency. It seems even pets have become Orykōsan lately.
Orykōsan, that's good enough. I'm done with the pet search.
Oh my, you've really risen up, haven't you? I won't be caring for you if you come crying later.
Ah.
Just kidding, sorry.
How's business?
It's going okay. There's no big profit, but I'm earning enough to live normally.
So you were just a mundane person, huh?
You’re not so detached from reality that you could live on just mist.
Did you manage to get a job that’s fitting for a detective?
Uh
m
I guess I just got promoted from pet to human.
Searching for someone? That sounds like it comes with a lot of trouble.
Yeah. But it seems like I'll wrap it up soon. When that's done, could you hook me up with some work again?
If you have any.
That is, if it’s okay.
Once I finish this job, let’s grab a drink.
It's a person who's full of mysteries. I’ve heard rumors he’s linked to the underworld.
He looks like a weak guy, but apparently, he’s skilled in karate, judo, and jiu-jitsu. Though I haven’t confirmed it.
He can say tough things with that face of his, ya know?
It's not directly related, but ever since it came up at the party, I’ve been curious.
Once Meryl's situation is settled, should we think about what the suspect looks like?
It was a pretty mysterious article.
Guy said he might have approved the article, but why would he have to do something like that?
Guy didn’t even get mad at Eva, who looked so confident.
Once Guy was gone, the article vanished too.
It's only a matter of time before Meryl's request is settled. So, does that mean I’ll have to go on another pet search?
I could live without working, but I don’t want to end up leading such a lazy life.
Don’t worry.
Anyway, for now, settling Meryl's job comes first.
※※※ It's a bug ※※※
